data.description:
## Summary
Reported by a session using the new auto-lander (epic f1fa). When the serial auto-lander rebases a change to the current `main` tip (because `main` advanced under it — the common concurrent case), the rebase mints a NEW patchset. S1 deliberately made the `Autosubmit` label **non-sticky** (empty `copyCondition`) so `addPatchSet` cannot forge a stale land intent (security). But that same non-stickiness means the **bot's own rebase DROPS `Autosubmit`** — so the change no longer matches the selection query `status:open label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` and falls out of the lander's pool mid-flight. Under concurrent traffic this stalls the automatic land and forces a manual rebase — defeating the 'set it and walk away' goal.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Set `Autosubmit+1` on a submittable change while other sessions are landing (so `main` advances).
2. The bot selects it, then `main` advances → the bot rebases to the new tip (new patchset).
3. The new patchset has NO `Autosubmit` (empty copyCondition drops it).
4. On the next poll / a re-drive / a bot restart, the change is not `label:Autosubmit+1` → NOT re-selected → the land stalls; the agent must rebase + re-apply Autosubmit manually.

Expected: the bot's own rebase preserves the land intent, so the change keeps being driven to terminal (merged / handed back) without human intervention.
Actual: the bot's rebase drops Autosubmit; the in-flight land is orphaned across re-drive/restart under concurrent traffic.

## Root cause (design tension)
S1's non-sticky `Autosubmit` (anti-forgery: addPatchSet must not carry a land intent) collides with S2's rebase drive (which mints a new patchset). Within a single in-memory drive the wipChain still holds the change so my dogfood (#618) landed; the gap bites across a re-drive / hand-back / bot restart, where selection re-queries and the dropped label orphans the change.

## Fix options (to design)
- **Bot re-applies `Autosubmit` after its own rebase** (it holds the change in wipChain and the requester's intent is known/authorized): re-set `Autosubmit+1` (under the bot's or the original requester's identity) immediately after a successful rebase, so the change stays selectable. Must preserve the anti-forgery property (only the LANDER re-applies, keyed to an in-flight wipChain — a random addPatchSet still cannot forge it).
- OR the lander tracks in-flight intent durably in its own state (wipChain persisted) so selection does not rely on the live label surviving the rebase — re-drive/restart reads the durable intent, not `label:Autosubmit+1`.
- Either way: preserve the S1 security invariant (a forged addPatchSet cannot self-request a land).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The bot's own rebase does NOT orphan an in-flight land: after a lander rebase, the change remains driven to a terminal outcome without a human re-applying Autosubmit — verified by a test simulating main-advances-then-rebase across a re-drive, and a live check under concurrent traffic.
- [ ] The S1 anti-forgery invariant is preserved (a third-party `addPatchSet` still cannot carry/forge `Autosubmit`); documented + a config-check or test asserting it.
- [ ] ADR-0042 / land-contract updated with the chosen intent-persistence design.

## Notes
Discovered while dogfooding f1fa (a separate session hit the manual-rebase stall). Related: S1 (843b) non-sticky label; S2b (a9e3) rebase drive; the FFO rebase-treadmill (ADR-0040).
data.title: Auto-lander: non-sticky Autosubmit dropped by the bot's OWN rebase stalls automatic landing under concurrent traffic
